talk_metadata.talk_title: Non-enzymatic HDAC-mediated repression reveals the selective stress adaptive mechanisms for cancer therapy

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: disrupting thousands of genes simultaneously due to the broad active site of HDAC paralogs and their involvement in multiple complexes.
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: thousands of genes are de-repressed all at once. So this is not a mere side effect. So you are causing a lot of cytotoxicity and off-target effect, because of which we have so less clinical success with the traditional enzymatic inhibitors.

key_data_gaps: experimental validation of p53 activation mechanism in mutant p53 cell lines
detailed mechanism of ATF4 activity in relation to SIN3 regulation

unresolved_questions: What is the exact mechanism by which E6R disrupts the Sin3 HDAC complex?
Is ATF4 activity a direct outcome of SIN3 regulation or an indirect effect?
What is the precise mechanism of p53 activation in mutant p53 neuroblastoma cell lines by E6R?
What are the specific mechanisms by which E6R impacts glycolysis?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Molecular glue degrader of the INT protein

key_data_gaps: detailed mechanism of ARNT degradation
specific mechanism of toxicity related to EMT or metabolic collapse

crispro_opportunity.description: NEO811 shows activity in acquired resistance to HIF-2 alpha inhibitors (G323E mutation) and intrinsic resistance (low HIF-2 alpha, high HIF-1 alpha).
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: NEO811 still retained full activity [in G323E mutation model]... NEO811 showed robust tumor growth inhibition, and again, a little bit better than just HIF-2 alpha alone. Again, these PDXs, they're serially transplanted over time. So we're doing the PD assessment on these, and they may now express a little HIF-2 compared to the publication, because again, belzutifan did show some activity. But we're very excited because NEO811 not only shows activity in acquired resistance to HIF-2 alpha inhibitors, but also intrinsic resistance, showing this pan-HIF activity may really be a driver in the clinic.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO's platform can identify pan-HIF activity and its implications in resistance settings, potentially guiding combination strategies.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: status of NEO811 trials in clear cell renal cell carcinoma
PubMed: further research on ARNT's role in intrinsic resistance to HIF-2 alpha inhibitors

crispro_opportunity.description: NEO811 shows combination effect with belzutifan and potential for combinations with other standards of care.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: So even though we are encouraged as the single agent studies, we wanted to then go in and say, can we do anything with combinations with other standard of cares, really to hit these pathways a little bit harder? And so giving the clinical success of HIF-2 alpha inhibitors, especially belzutifan recently, we wanted to test NEO811 and belzutifan in combination... not only adding a HIF-2 alpha inhibitor with ARNT, so they're targeting the exact same pathway, is that hopefully in the future, this will open us up to other combinations of other standards of care.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can analyze synergistic effects of NEO811 with other standards of care, identifying optimal combination partners for CRCC.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: ongoing trials for belzutifan in CRCC
Company pipeline databases: status of other HIF-2 alpha inhibitors

unresolved_questions: What is the specific degron for ARNT targeted by NEO811?
What is the mechanism by which NEO811's pan-HIF activity leads to tumor regression (e.g., vascularization, immune milieu changes, metabolic collapse)?
How does NEO811's selectivity for ARNT over ARNT2 translate to clinical efficacy and safety?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Molecular glue degrader targeting the CCNE1

key_data_gaps: clinical development strategy and biomarkers beyond CCNE1 amplification
long-term durability of PD response in vivo
mechanism of resistance to CDK2 inhibitors in CCNE1 amplified cells

crispro_opportunity.description: While CCNE1 amplification is a strong biomarker, some amplified cell lines show reduced sensitivity, potentially due to P16 expression or other factors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Yes. So one interesting phenomenon is that cyclin E1 amplified cancers are almost always P16 high, so they express an endogenous inhibitor of CDK4/6. So it appears that, and there's also evidence for that, that there is a downside of having both hyperactive CDK2 and active CDK4/6 on top of it. So it's either/or in most cases. There was, I think in this panel, two exceptions, which had low P16, and this explains that then CDK4/6 is active at the same time as CDK2 and pure cyclin E1 degradation doesn't work.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can analyze the interplay of CCNE1 amplification, P16 expression, and CDK4/6 activity to refine patient stratification for MRT55811.
external_validation_needed: TCGA data analysis: correlation of CCNE1 amplification, P16 expression, and CDK4/6 activity in various cancer types
ClinicalTrials.gov: patient selection criteria for MRT55811 trials

crispro_opportunity.description: The CCNE1 degrader shows activity in models resistant to CDK2 inhibitors, suggesting a potential to overcome resistance to existing therapies.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: CDK2 inhibitors, interestingly, are a bit different. So they do show preferential activity for cyclin E1 amplified cell lines, and that's more obvious in the lower plot where you can see the GI50s. But there are significant overlaps, so there's also a lot of activity in cell lines that are not amplified. We have seen this with multiple CDK2 inhibitors, not all are shown here.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can identify patient populations that have failed or are resistant to CDK2 inhibitors but may benefit from CCNE1 degradation.
external_validation_needed: Clinical trial data: response rates of CDK2 inhibitors in CCNE1 amplified cancers
Biomarker analysis: identify patients with CCNE1 amplification who are refractory to CDK2 inhibitors

cited_competitors.name: CDK2 inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: used for comparison, show preferential activity for CCNE1 amplified cell lines

unresolved_questions: What is the precise mechanism by which MRT55811 co-degrades CDK2 and phospho-CDK2?
What are the long-term durability and safety profiles of MRT55811 in vivo?
What are the specific biomarkers beyond CCNE1 amplification that predict sensitivity to MRT55811?
Does MRT55811 degrade cyclin E2?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Molecular glue degrader of the RNA binding protein HuR

key_data_gaps: mechanism of HuR activation in BRAF mutant CRC
specific mechanism of resistance to BRAF inhibitors
detailed mechanism of interferon signaling induction by HuR degrader

crispro_opportunity.description: HuR dependency is strongly correlated with BRAF mutations, suggesting BRAF mutant CRC as a key indication, but other BRAF mutant cancers may also be sensitive.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Analysis of that data showed a strong and positive correlation between HUR dependency and BRAF. Since BRAF mutations are prevalent in skin and colorectal cancers, we suppose that a BRAF mutant colorectal cancer might be particularly sensitive to HUR degradation.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can analyze the correlation between HuR dependency and BRAF mutations across various cancer types to identify additional indications for HuR degraders.
external_validation_needed: TCGA data analysis: correlation of HuR expression/dependency and BRAF mutations in melanoma, lung cancer, and other relevant tumor types
ClinicalTrials.gov: enrollment criteria for DG6498 trials

crispro_opportunity.description: HuR degrader shows comparable efficacy to BRAF inhibitors in BRAF-mutant CRC, including dabrafenib-resistant models, suggesting it can overcome or bypass resistance.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: BRAF dabrafenib-resistant MDSDN cells were generated through long-time exposure. As expected, these resistant cells showed a clear loss of sensitivity to dabrafenib compared with this parental compound. In contrast, when treated with our HUR degrader Both parental compound and dabrafenib-resistant cells displayed strong and comparable reductions in cell viabilities.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can identify patients with BRAF-mutant CRC who have developed resistance to BRAF inhibitors and may benefit from HuR degradation therapy.
external_validation_needed: Clinical trial data: response rates of BRAF inhibitors in BRAF-mutant CRC patients
Biomarker analysis: identify patients with BRAF-mutant CRC refractory to BRAF inhibitors

crispro_opportunity.description: The HuR degrader induces interferon signaling, suggesting potential for combination with immune-oncology therapies.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: This increase in interferon signaling then leads to increased secretion of chemokines and cytokines. In this model, we can see an increase in CCL5 and CXCL10, which of course, are important chemoattractants for T cells in the tumor microenvironment.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can analyze the immune-modulatory effects of HuR degraders and identify synergistic combinations with immunotherapies.
external_validation_needed: Preclinical studies: evaluate combination of HuR degrader with checkpoint inhibitors or CAR-T therapies in relevant models
Clinical trial data: immune cell infiltration and response in patients treated with HuR degraders

What are the specific downstream targets of HuR that are critical for BRAF mutant CRC survival and resistance?

key_data_gaps: precise mechanism of how BB010203 interferes with RS2-HER2 complex formation
long-term efficacy and safety of BB010203 in combination therapies
activity of BB010203 in HER2 mutant models

crispro_opportunity.description: BB010203 shows strong combination potential with HER2-targeted agents (tucatinib, trastuzumab) in HER2-amplified models, potentially overcoming shortcomings of existing therapies.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: So to test that in BT474 cell lines, which is a HER2-amplified model, we gave the combined approach of 10203 in combination with tucatinib here on the left or trastuzumab here on the right. And what you can see in blue is strong monotherapy effects with 10203. But when you combine that with the other agent, it led to strong regressions... And when given in combination with the HER2 targeting antibody trastuzumab, led to strong regressions, and again, outperformed the combination of tucatinib plus trastuzumab.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can analyze the synergistic effects of BB010203 with HER2-targeted agents and other therapies in HER2-amplified cancers to optimize combination strategies.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: status of Breaker-101 clinical trial
Company pipeline databases: development status of BB010203 and its combinations

crispro_opportunity.description: The disruption of non-canonical RAS signaling by BB010203 may address resistance mechanisms to canonical RAS inhibitors and HER2-targeted therapies.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: So this data shows by genetic mechanisms that the PIK3CA RAS interaction is essential for phospho-AKT activation and survival. So to understand what RAS isoforms were driving this dependency, the first thing we did is we screened our panel of our two amplified lines against with 10203 compared to the pan-RAS inhibitor 6236. And what we observed is that with 10203, as expected, we saw complete phospho-AKT suppression. But with the pan-RAS inhibitor RMC-6236, we saw moderate suppression of phospho-AKT, despite seeing complete phospho-ERK suppression. This was seen across a larger panel of lines, here shown on the right. So this data to us indicated that the phospho-AKT activity in these lines was not likely driven from canonical RAS proteins, which were inhibited by the RMC-6236, but likely coming from non-canonical proteins, which were not hit by 6236.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can identify resistance mechanisms to HER2-targeted therapies and canonical RAS inhibitors that are driven by non-canonical RAS signaling, and assess BB010203's potential to overcome them.
external_validation_needed: PubMed: research on resistance mechanisms to HER2-targeted therapies and canonical RAS inhibitors
Clinical trial data: identify patients with HER2-amplified cancers who have progressed on standard therapies

unresolved_questions: What is the precise mechanism by which BB010203 disrupts the RS2-HER2 complex?
What is the role of canonical RAS proteins in driving phospho-AKT signaling in HER2-amplified lines, and how does BB010203 inhibit this component?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Catalytic inhibitor of the KAT2A/B protein

key_data_gaps: mechanism of interferon signaling induction by KAT2 inhibition
therapeutic window between normal and cancer cells, given SAGA's role in normal development
specific roles of KAT2A vs. KAT2B in multiple myeloma and other cancers

crispro_opportunity.description: KAT2 inhibition upregulates interferon responses, suggesting potential for combination with immune-oncology therapies like BCMA CD3 bispecific antibodies.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: So we then utilized a mouse syngeneic model of multiple myeloma to test tumor growth inhibition in combination with BCMA CD3 bispecific antibody. ... But additionally, what you can see also is that we do extend the tumor growth delay in a dose-dependent manner when we add our KAT2 inhibitor with the bispecific antibody. And this is reflected in the survival curve shown on the right. So we're very excited about the idea that this could potentially open up opportunities to combine our KAT2 inhibitor with IO therapies to deepen responses in multiple myeloma.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can analyze the immune-modulatory effects of KAT2 inhibitors and identify synergistic combinations with T-cell engagers and other immunotherapies.
external_validation_needed: Clinical trial data: immune cell infiltration and response in patients treated with KAT2 inhibitors
Preclinical studies: evaluate combination of KAT2 inhibitor with checkpoint inhibitors or CAR-T therapies in relevant models

crispro_opportunity.description: KAT2 inhibition shows synergistic activity with IMiDs (lenalidomide, pomalidomide) in multiple myeloma, suggesting it can enhance the efficacy of standard of care treatments.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: So we combined our KAT2 inhibitor with either lenalidomide or pomalidomide in a combination matrix experiment for proliferation in vitro. ... So when you get this output data, if you get a value between zero to one, this is considered additive, and you can see this by the column on the right where we have the self-cross of our compound with itself and those values range there. If you have a value that's greater than one, this suggests you can have synergy between the two compounds, and you can see for both our KAT2 inhibitor combination with lenalidomide and pomalidomide, most of the cell lines we observe synergistic scores.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can identify patient populations with multiple myeloma who may benefit from combination therapy with KAT2 inhibitors and IMiDs.
external_validation_needed: Clinical trial data: response rates of IMiDs in multiple myeloma patients
Biomarker analysis: identify patients with multiple myeloma who are refractory or have limited response to IMiDs

crispro_opportunity.description: The SAGA complex and KAT2A/B are identified as top dependencies for multiple myeloma, suggesting a potential for biomarker-driven patient selection.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: So what you're seeing here is this is a bit of the DepMap data where we were asking in multiple myeloma cells versus every other tumor type, what are the protein complexes that myeloma cells are most dependent on? And so we used the CRISPR dropout scores here. We used a quorum protein complex analysis, and what you can see here is that the SAGA complex and other KAT2-containing complexes showed up as the top dependencies for multiple myeloma, as are highlighted here in the red.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can analyze the dependency of various cancer types on SAGA and KAT2A/B to identify optimal indications and biomarkers for KAT2 inhibitors.
external_validation_needed: DepMap data analysis: comprehensive assessment of KAT2A/B dependency across all cancer types
Clinical trial data: correlation of KAT2A/B expression or dependency with response to KAT2 inhibitors

unresolved_questions: What is the precise mechanism by which KAT2 inhibition leads to the upregulation of interferon signaling?
What is the therapeutic window for KAT2 inhibitors, considering the role of SAGA in normal development?
What are the specific roles of KAT2A versus KAT2B in driving oncogenesis in multiple myeloma and other cancer types?
Does the SAGA complex have other dependencies that could be targeted in combination with KAT2 inhibitors?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Discovery of a new class of mutant-targeted catalytic RAS oncogene inhibitor

key_data_gaps: stoichiometry and catalytic efficacy of RMO-55 in cellular contexts
long-term toxicity profile of RMO-55 compared to deraxone rasib
activity of RMO-55 against G13D and Q61H RAS mutants
mechanism of resistance to RMO-55

crispro_opportunity.description: RMO-55 demonstrates activity in models of acquired resistance to RAS on inhibitors, particularly those involving KRAS amplification or increased expression.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: So going back to the clinical resistance I started with, one of the major mechanisms of clinical resistance, at least in terms of where we can detect a mechanism of resistance from genomic alterations in ctDNA, is KRAS amplification of the mutant associated with the G12 mutant allele... However, RMO-55 at 10 MPK was able to produce deep and very durable regressions in these resistant models. In a related model, we looked at the ability of RMO-55 to restore tumor control in tumors that were already outgrowing on deraxone rasib. And you can see here, these tumors initially showed regression, but then started to grow out with continued deraxone rasib treatment. However, in the group that we switched over to RMO-55, we were able to restore tumor control and had durable regressions out to the end of the study.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can identify resistance mechanisms to RAS inhibitors, including those involving RAS amplification or overexpression, and assess RMO-55's potential to overcome them.
external_validation_needed: Clinical trial data: identify patients with RAS-mutant cancers who have progressed on RAS inhibitors
PubMed: research on resistance mechanisms to RAS inhibitors, including amplification and overexpression

crispro_opportunity.description: RMO-55's catalytic mechanism and potential for broader RAS inhibition suggest opportunities for combination with other RAS inhibitors or therapies targeting downstream effectors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: So the G13 and some of the Q61, we have steric occlusion activity analogous to the steric occlusion activity that I described at wild type. Mm-hmm. Okay. Thank you. A very nice talk. So you show that the RM-055 has stimulate the GTP hydrolysis compared to daxonesib. So compared to direct combo with the RAS-off inhibitor, does this RM-055 provide the advantage for the combo with the RAS-off inhibitor? We are currently looking at a number of different combinations, and that is obviously one we're interested in.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can analyze the synergistic effects of RMO-55 with RAS-off inhibitors and other therapies targeting the RAS pathway to optimize combination strategies.
external_validation_needed: Clinical trial data: response rates of RAS-off inhibitors in RAS-mutant cancers
Preclinical studies: evaluate combination of RMO-55 with RAS-off inhibitors in relevant models

crispro_opportunity.description: RMO-55 represents a new class of RAS inhibitor with a catalytic mechanism, offering a distinct approach compared to existing RAS on and RAS off inhibitors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Thus far, there's really been two major classes of RAS inhibitor. The first class is the RAS off inhibitors. These are switch two pocket binders... The other class of inhibitors is the molecular glues or tricomplex inhibitors. These bind exclusively to the on state of RAS... Today, I would like to introduce a third class of inhibitors. Oh, my goodness. That basically catalytically inactivate RAS and then essentially stimulate the hydrolysis of RAS GTP to RAS GDP and mutant RAS, and in so doing, mimic the activity of natural gaps at wild-type RAS.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can highlight the unique catalytic mechanism of RMO-55 and its potential advantages over existing RAS inhibitor classes in addressing resistance and unmet needs.
external_validation_needed: Market analysis: competitive landscape of RAS inhibitors
Clinical trial data: comparative efficacy and safety of RMO-55 versus existing RAS inhibitors

cited_competitors.context: RAS on inhibitor, used for comparison and resistance studies

cited_competitors.name: cyclophilin
cited_competitors.context: endogenous chaperone protein involved in RAS on inhibitors

unresolved_questions: What is the precise mechanism by which RMO-55 stimulates GTP hydrolysis in G12 mutant RAS proteins?
What is the stoichiometry of RMO-55's catalytic activity in cellular contexts?
What are the specific resistance mechanisms to RMO-55, and how can they be overcome?
What is the activity of RMO-55 against other RAS mutations, such as G13D and Q61H?
